recalls.Description: Luyuan has issued a recall for the X-Pro Eagle 40cc Youth ATV and the FRP Sahara 40cc Youth ATV due to safety concerns. These ATVs, available in multiple colors, are identified by "X PRO" labels on the sides and "FRP" labels on the front and sides. The recall is prompted by the vehicles' failure to meet mandatory safety standards, which could lead to severe injury or even death in the event of a crash.
recalls.Title: Luyuan Recalls X-Pro Eagle and FRP Sahara Youth ATVs Due to Serious Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Luyuan at 855-663-2121, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at luyuanusa@gmail.com or visit www.luyuanusa.com and click on "Recall" for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled X-PRO ATV Eagle 40cc (front view)
Recalled X-PRO ATV Eagle 40cc (side view)
Location of X-PRO label on both sides of seat and plastic body
Recalled FRP ATV Sahara 40cc (red)
Recalled FRP ATV Sahara 40cc (blue)
Recalled FRP ATV Sahara 40cc (black)
Location of FRP label on the front plastic cover and both sides of plastic body

Manufacturers.Name: Yongkang Luyuan Industrial & Trading Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: X-Pro Eagle 40cc Youth ATVs were sold online at Walmart.com, Powersportsmax.com, and Xprousa.com from June 2025 through September 2025 for between $480 and $500. FRP Sahara 40cc Youth ATVs were sold online at Amazon.com, Temu.com, and Frpmoto.com from April 2025 through September 2025 for between $460 and $480.

Importers.Name: Luyuan Inc., of City of Industry, California

Hazards.Name: The recalled youth ATVs do not comply with mandatory safety standards, presenting a crash hazard due to mechanical suspension issues. Additionally, the handlebars can cause lacerations if a rider impacts them at high speed, leading to a risk of serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the X-Pro Eagle 40cc and FRP Sahara 40cc Youth ATVs immediately and contact Luyuan for a complimentary repair at an authorized service center.

seo.metaDescription: Luyuan recalls X-Pro Eagle and FRP Sahara Youth ATVs due to serious safety risks. Immediate action is required for consumer safety.
seo.ogTitle: Luyuan Recalls X-Pro Eagle and FRP Sahara Youth ATVs Due to Serious Injury Risks
seo.ogDescription: Luyuan recalls X-Pro Eagle and FRP Sahara Youth ATVs due to serious safety risks. Immediate action is required for consumer safety.
seo.twitterTitle: Luyuan Recalls X-Pro Eagle and FRP Sahara Youth ATVs Due to Serious Injury Risks
seo.twitterDescription: Luyuan recalls X-Pro Eagle and FRP Sahara Youth ATVs due to serious safety risks. Immediate action is required for consumer safety.

recalls.Description: 509 has issued a recall for several models of Tactical helmets, including the Tactical 3.0 and Tactical 2.0, due to inadequate protection in crash scenarios, which could lead to head injuries. The affected helmets include various sizes and colors, manufactured between 2020 and 2023. Helmets branded with the 509 logo and 'FIVE O NINE' or 'Polaris' are part of this recall. Consumers are urged to check if their helmet is affected and take appropriate action.
recalls.Title: 509 Recalls Tactical Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact 509 at 877-743-3509 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email support@ride509.com. More details can be found at https://ride509.com/pages/recalls. For Polaris inquiries, call 800-765-2747 during the same hours or visit www.polaris.com and click on 'Product Safety News'. You can also reach Polaris' owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled 509 Tactical 3.0 Youth Helmet
Recalled 509 Tactical 3.0 Mountain Helmet
Recalled 509 Tactical 3.0 Trail Helmet
Recalled 509 for Polaris Tactical 3.0 Youth Helmet
Recalled 509 for Polaris Tactical 3.0 Mountain Helmet
Recalled 509 Tactical 2.0 Small/Medium Helmets
Recalled 509 for Polaris Tactical 2.0 Small/Medium Helmets
Recalled 509 and 509 for Polaris Helmets with Fidlock®
Correct Fidlock
Incorrect Fidlock Strap Fastener

Injuries.Name: The firm received one incident where the rider's helmet became detached during a crash, resulting in a concussion.

Retailers.Name: Online at www.ride509.com and www.polaris.com and at 509 and Polaris dealers nationwide from January 2020 through May 2025 for between $140 and $900.

Importers.Name: Teton Outfitters LLC, dba 509, a Polaris subsidiary, of Rigby, Idaho

Hazards.Name: Insufficient protection leading to head injury risk

Remedies.Name: Replacement, refund, or credit options for 509 Tactical helmets

seo.metaDescription: 509 recalls Tactical helmets due to head injury risks. Check if your helmet is affected and learn how to get a refund or replacement.
seo.ogTitle: 509 Recalls Tactical Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk
seo.ogDescription: 509 recalls Tactical helmets due to head injury risks. Check if your helmet is affected and learn how to get a refund or replacement.
seo.twitterTitle: 509 Recalls Tactical Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk
seo.twitterDescription: 509 recalls Tactical helmets due to head injury risks. Check if your helmet is affected and learn how to get a refund or replacement.

recalls.Description: Lil Pick Up is recalling the E-Bully Youth Electric 1000W ATVs, which have been sold under various brand names like 'Seangles' and 'Offroad Mall.' These ATVs can be identified by the model name 'E-Bully' on the VIN plate located on the right front side of the frame column. A warning plate on the left front side indicates that these ATVs are part of an action plan approved by the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission. They are available in several colors, including blue, green, white, red, and black.
recalls.Title: Lil Pick Up Recalls E-Bully Youth ATVs Due to Serious Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Lil Pick Up at 805-570-4860 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email contact@lilpickup.us. Additional details can be found online at www.lilpickup.us/recall-detail.html or by visiting www.lilpickup.us and selecting 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled E-Bully Youth ATV
Recalled Offroad Mall E-Bully Youth ATV
Recalled E-Bully Youth ATV VIN Plate

Manufacturers.Name: Shandong ODES Industry Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Multiple online retailers, including Amazon.com, Ebay.com, Seangles.com and other websites from September 2023 through October 2024 for about $1,000.

Importers.Name: Lil Pick Up Inc., of Rowland Heights, California

Hazards.Name: The recalled youth ATVs do not meet federal ATV safety standards, posing laceration risks from handlebars and collision hazards due to ineffective parking brakes, leading to potential serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately cease using the recalled ATVs and contact Lil Pick Up for a complimentary repair at an authorized service center.

seo.metaDescription: Lil Pick Up recalls E-Bully Youth ATVs due to serious injury risks. Stop using them and contact for a free repair.
seo.ogTitle: Lil Pick Up Recalls E-Bully Youth ATVs Due to Serious Injury Risks

recalls.Description: Honda has issued a recall for accessory heaters designed for specific model years of their Pioneer and Talon side-by-side vehicles. This recall affects the 2023-2025 Honda Pioneer 700, 2022-2024 Pioneer 1000, and 2022-2024 Talon 1000 models. These heaters, sold separately, come with a control panel that allows users to adjust air speed, airflow direction, and temperature. To check if your vehicle is affected, visit https://powersports.honda.com/recalls and enter your VIN.
recalls.Title: Honda Recalls Accessory Heaters for Pioneer and Talon Vehicles Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact American Honda at 866-784-1870 from 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://powersports.honda.com and click on 'Recall Information' for further details.

Products.Name: Accessory Heaters for 2022-2024 Honda Pioneer 1000, 2023-2025 Pioneer 700, and 2022-2024 Honda Talon 1000

Images.Caption: Recalled accessory heater system and control panel
Compatible Honda Pioneer and Honda Talon vehicles

Injuries.Name: Honda has received three reports of the recalled accessory heaters overheating, including two incidents resulting in fires and vehicle damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Authorized Honda Powersports dealers nationwide from January 2022 through September 2024 for between $900 and $1,040.

Distributors.Name: American Honda Motor Co. Inc, of Torrance, California

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating

Remedies.Name: Stop using the heaters and contact an authorized Honda dealer for a free repair

seo.metaDescription: Honda recalls accessory heaters for Pioneer and Talon vehicles due to fire hazards. Check your VIN for compatibility and contact Honda for repairs.
seo.ogTitle: Honda Recalls Accessory Heaters for Pioneer and Talon Vehicles Due to Fire Risks
seo.ogDescription: Honda recalls accessory heaters for Pioneer and Talon vehicles due to fire hazards. Check your VIN for compatibility and contact Honda for repairs.
seo.twitterTitle: Honda Recalls Accessory Heaters for Pioneer and Talon Vehicles Due to Fire Risks
seo.twitterDescription: Honda recalls accessory heaters for Pioneer and Talon vehicles due to fire hazards. Check your VIN for compatibility and contact Honda for repairs.

recalls.Description: SuperATV is recalling specific carrier bearings designed for Polaris RZR XP Turbo and Turbo S recreational off-highway vehicles, sold between April 2017 and September 2022. These carrier bearings, identifiable by their silver and black coloring and 'SuperATV' engraving on the housing, can pose serious fire hazards if not addressed. The affected models include BEA01-001, BEA01-002, and BEA01-005, purchased within specified date ranges. The carrier bearings are located beneath the center console tunnel of the vehicles.
recalls.Title: SuperATV Recalls Carrier Bearings for Polaris RZR XP and Turbo S Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact SuperATV at 855-728-8256 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also visit https://form.jotform.com/243023918871055 to verify if your part is affected, or check https://www.superatv.com/recalls for more details.

Products.Name: SuperATV Carrier Bearings for Polaris RZR XP Turbo and Turbo S

Images.Caption: Recalled SuperATV Carrier Bearing Model BEA01-001
Recalled SuperATV Carrier Bearing Model BEA01-002
Recalled SuperATV Carrier Bearing Model BEA01-005 (Rev A00 and A01)
Location of SuperATV Brand Name on Carrier Bearing

Injuries.Name: SuperATV has received 24 reports of the fuel tank contacting the prop shaft, including 19 reports that a hole or crack had formed in the fuel tank, including one reported fire. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Online at www.superatv.com and at off-road vehicle dealers nationwide from April 2017 until September 2022 for between $85 and $250 (for one carrier bearing) and between $170 and $420 (set of 2 carrier bearings).

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to potential fuel tank leakage

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled carrier bearings, remove them, and register online for a return label to receive free replacements.

seo.metaDescription: SuperATV recalls carrier bearings for Polaris RZR XP Turbo and Turbo S due to fire hazards. Check if your part is affected and get replacements.
seo.ogTitle: SuperATV Recalls Carrier Bearings for Polaris RZR XP and Turbo S Due to Fire Risks
seo.ogDescription: SuperATV recalls carrier bearings for Polaris RZR XP Turbo and Turbo S due to fire hazards. Check if your part is affected and get replacements.
seo.twitterTitle: SuperATV Recalls Carrier Bearings for Polaris RZR XP and Turbo S Due to Fire Risks
seo.twitterDescription: SuperATV recalls carrier bearings for Polaris RZR XP Turbo and Turbo S due to fire hazards. Check if your part is affected and get replacements.

recalls.Description: Polaris Industries has announced a recall for the 2023-2024 Model Year Ranger XP Kinetic Recreational Off-Road Vehicles (ROVs). These vehicles, available in camouflage and white, feature a three-seat layout and display the "POLARIS" logo on the front grille. The model number and VIN can be found on the left rear frame beneath the cargo box on the driver's side.
recalls.Title: Polaris Industries Issues Recall for Ranger XP Kinetic Off-Road Vehicles Due to Fire and Crash Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747, available from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. For more information, visit www.polaris.com and navigate to "Product Safety News" in the "Safety" section at the bottom of the page, or check https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/ to see if your vehicle's VIN is affected. Additionally, Polaris' owner connections team can be contacted at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online help center at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic Recreational Off-Road Vehicles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,400 (In addition, about 70 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Polaris Model Year 2023 RANGER XP Kinetic Recreational Off-Road Vehicle – White
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2023 RANGER XP Kinetic Recreational Off-Road Vehicle – Camouflage
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2024 RANGER XP Kinetic Recreational Off-Road Vehicle – White
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2024 RANGER XP Kinetic Recreational Off-Road Vehicle – Camouflage
Recalled Polaris RANGER VIN Location

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of unintended acceleration and fire and six reports of melting on or around the motor controller unit. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Polaris Industries Inc., of Medina, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Polaris dealers nationwide from May 2023 through September 2024 for between $24,900 and $37,800.

Hazards.Name: Loose high voltage cables on the motor controller unit may lead to arcing or overheating, creating fire and crash hazards, which could result in serious injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled ROVs and contact an authorized Polaris dealer for a complimentary inspection of the motor controller unit. If any high voltage cables are found to be loose or damaged, Polaris will arrange for a free repair at the dealer's location. Registered owners will be contacted directly by Polaris.

seo.metaDescription: Polaris recalls Ranger XP Kinetic ROVs due to fire and crash hazards. Stop using immediately and contact an authorized dealer for inspection.
seo.ogTitle: Polaris Industries Issues Recall for Ranger XP Kinetic Off-Road Vehicles Due to Fire and Crash Risks
seo.ogDescription: Polaris recalls Ranger XP Kinetic ROVs due to fire and crash hazards. Stop using immediately and contact an authorized dealer for inspection.
seo.twitterTitle: Polaris Industries Issues Recall for Ranger XP Kinetic Off-Road Vehicles Due to Fire and Crash Risks
seo.twitterDescription: Polaris recalls Ranger XP Kinetic ROVs due to fire and crash hazards. Stop using immediately and contact an authorized dealer for inspection.

recalls.Description: Ricky Powersports is recalling its Tumble Weed youth ATVs, model TK-110ATV-1, and TGB Blade 600 SE adult ATVs due to significant safety concerns. The Tumble Weed models, available in various colors, are designed for children aged 6 and older, while the TGB Blade 600 SE is intended for adults aged 16 and up. Each model features an identification plate that confirms compliance with Ricky Powersports LLC's action plan approved by the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission.
recalls.Title: Ricky Powersports Recalls Youth and Adult ATVs Due to Serious Injury Risks and Safety Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Ricky Powersports toll-free at 844-250-2199 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email at RickyPowerSports@gmail.com, or visit www.Rickypowersports.net/Recall or www.RickyPowerSports.net and click on the "RECALL" button.

Products.Name: Ricky Powersports Tumble Weed Youth ATV

Images.Caption: Recalled Ricky Powersports RPS TK110ATV-1 Tumble Weed Youth ATV
The youth ATV's model number appears on a plate located at the front of the vehicle between the two front tires
Recalled Ricky Powersports RPS Blade 600 SE Adult ATV
The adult ATV's model number appears on a plate located next to the vehicle's front right tire
Identification plates on both the youth and the adult models

Manufacturers.Name: Jiangsu Tiking Sports Vehicle Manufacturing Co. Ltd., of China (youth ATV)
Taiwan Golden Bee Co. Ltd., of Taiwan (adult ATV)

Retailers.Name: Ricky Powersports dealers nationwide from January 2021 through May 2024 for between $850 and $1,100 for the youth model, and for about $9,300 for the adult model.

Importers.Name: Ricky Powersports LLC, of Carrollton, Texas

Hazards.Name: The recalled ATVs do not meet federal ATV safety regulations. The youth models have a mechanical suspension that poses a crash risk, while the adult models' brakes may fail to stop the vehicle adequately, also creating a crash hazard. Additionally, the handlebars could cause lacerations if impacted by the rider's body or head, increasing the risk of serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled ATVs immediately and contact Ricky Powersports to arrange for a full refund.

seo.metaDescription: Ricky Powersports recalls youth and adult ATVs due to serious injury risks. Stop using them and contact for a refund.
seo.ogTitle: Ricky Powersports Recalls Youth and Adult ATVs Due to Serious Injury Risks and Safety Regulation Violations
seo.ogDescription: Ricky Powersports recalls youth and adult ATVs due to serious injury risks. Stop using them and contact for a refund.
seo.twitterTitle: Ricky Powersports Recalls Youth and Adult ATVs Due to Serious Injury Risks and Safety Regulation Violations
seo.twitterDescription: Ricky Powersports recalls youth and adult ATVs due to serious injury risks. Stop using them and contact for a refund.

recalls.Description: CFMOTO is recalling its 2024 CFORCE 800 Touring and 2024 CFORCE 1000 Touring all-terrain vehicles (ATVs) due to a serious safety concern. These recreational vehicles, available in gray, orange, black, and camouflage colors, may experience a throttle lever malfunction. This issue can prevent the throttle from returning to the idle position, leading to unintended acceleration and potential crash risks. The Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) can be found on the frame behind the right rear wheel for identification purposes.
recalls.Title: CFMOTO Recalls 2024 CFORCE 800 and 1000 Touring ATVs Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact CFMOTO Powersports at 888-823-6686, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, reach out via email at info@cfmotousa.com or visit https://www.cfmotousa.com/vehicle-recall or www.CFMOTOUSA.com and navigate to "More" and then "Vehicle Recall" for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled CFMOTO CFORCE 800 Touring/1000 Touring ATV ROV (camo)
Recalled CFMOTO CFORCE 800 Touring/1000 Touring ATV ROV (gray)
Recalled CFMOTO CFORCE 800 Touring ATV ROV (orange)
Recalled CFMOTO CFORCE 1000 Touring ATV ROV (black)
VIN Location on the recalled 2024 CForce 800 Touring ATV

Injuries.Name: CFMOTO has received four reports of the throttle failing to return to the idle position and including one report of the vehicle crashing, including one minor impact injury.

Retailers.Name: CFMOTO Powersports dealers nationwide from May 2024 through August 2024 for between $9,400 and $10,500.

Importers.Name: CFMOTO Powersports Inc., of Plymouth, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Throttle lever may not return to idle, causing unexpected acceleration and crash risk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled ATVs and contact an authorized CFMOTO dealer for a free repair. CFMOTO has issued a stop ride notice and is reaching out to all registered owners and dealers.

seo.metaDescription: CFMOTO recalls 2024 CFORCE 800 and 1000 Touring ATVs due to throttle lever issues causing crash hazards. Contact for repairs.
seo.ogTitle: CFMOTO Recalls 2024 CFORCE 800 and 1000 Touring ATVs Due to Safety Risks
seo.ogDescription: CFMOTO recalls 2024 CFORCE 800 and 1000 Touring ATVs due to throttle lever issues causing crash hazards. Contact for repairs.
seo.twitterTitle: CFMOTO Recalls 2024 CFORCE 800 and 1000 Touring ATVs Due to Safety Risks
seo.twitterDescription: CFMOTO recalls 2024 CFORCE 800 and 1000 Touring ATVs due to throttle lever issues causing crash hazards. Contact for repairs.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to the Droyd Fury Youth All-Terrain Vehicle (ATV), model number 'XW-A01', designed for children aged 6 and above. These ATVs are available in three vibrant colors: Speed Red, Power Blue, and Force Green. The model name 'FURY' is prominently displayed on the front and sides, with the model number located on the identification plate beneath the front left fender. This plate also indicates that the ATV is part of the Jinling International, Inc. ATV Action Plan, which has received approval from the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission.
recalls.Title: Reyhee Group and Jingling International Recall Droyd Fury Youth ATVs Due to Laceration Risk and Federal Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Reyhee Group at 800-333-3729 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email support@go-droyd.com, or visit www.droyd.com/pages/safety-recall or www.droyd.com and select 'Safety Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Droyd Fury Youth ATV
Recalled Droyd Fury Youth ATV (side view)
Recalled Droyd Fury Youth ATV Identification Plate

Manufacturers.Name: Yongkang Ruihe Metal Products Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Online at Droyd.com, Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Target.com, Purchasingpower.com, Wellbots.com and Rideextremedirt.com from May 2024 through June 2024 for about $600.

Importers.Name: Reyhee Group Inc., dba RGI Global, of Ontario, California

Distributors.Name: Jingling International Inc., of Jasper, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Laceration risk due to non-compliance with federal ATV regulations; handlebars can cause injury upon impact.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled ATVs immediately and contact Reyhee Group for a complimentary repair kit, which includes a replacement handlebar cover and updated safety warning labels.

seo.metaDescription: Reyhee Group recalls Droyd Fury Youth ATVs due to laceration risks. Learn how to get a free repair kit and ensure safety.
seo.ogTitle: Reyhee Group and Jingling International Recall Droyd Fury Youth ATVs Due to Laceration Risk and Federal Regulation Violations

seo.twitterDescription: Reyhee Group recalls Droyd Fury Youth ATVs due to laceration risks. Learn how to get a free repair kit and ensure safety.

recalls.Description: Polaris is recalling specific VINs of its 2023-2024 Sportsman 850, XP 1000, and XP 1000 S all-terrain vehicles (ATVs), available in various colors including black, blue, and camouflage. Each ATV features the 'POLARIS' logo on the front grille and sides, with the model name also displayed on the chassis. The vehicle identification number (VIN) can be found on the left rear frame of the ATV. Additionally, this recall includes fuel pump kits (part numbers 2208595 and 2208596) and fuel tank assemblies (part numbers 2522378 and 2522379) sold as service parts.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls 2023-2024 Sportsman ATVs and Fuel Components Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Visit www.polaris.com and navigate to 'Product Safety News' under the 'Safety' section, or go to https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/ to verify if your VIN is affected. For further inquiries, contact Polaris' owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2023-2024 Polaris Sportsman 850, XP 1000, XP 1000 S ATVs and Fuel Components
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 10,300 ATVs and 3,158 fuel pump kits and fuel tank assemblies (In addition, about 1,500 ATVs were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Polaris Model Year 2023 Sportsman 850 ATV
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2023 Sportsman XP 1000 ATV
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2023 Sportsman XP 1000 S ATV
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2024 Sportsman 850 ATV
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2024 Sportsman XP 1000 ATV
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2024 Sportsman XP 1000 S ATV
Recalled Fuel Pump Kit (part numbers 2208595 and 2208596)
Recalled Fuel Tank Assembly (part numbers 2522378 and 2522379)
Recalled Polaris Sportsman ATVs VIN Location (1)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 14 reports of fuel leaking from the recalled ATVs and three reports of fire. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Polaris Industries Inc., of Medina, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Polaris dealers nationwide from July 2023 through June 2024 for between $10,600 and $18,000.

Hazards.Name: Potential fuel leak at the pump flange outlet, creating a fire hazard and risk of injury.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled ATVs immediately and contact an authorized Polaris dealer for a free fuel pump replacement. Polaris will reach out to registered owners directly.

seo.metaDescription: Polaris recalls 2023-2024 Sportsman ATVs due to fire risks. Check if your vehicle is affected and learn how to get a free repair.
seo.ogTitle: Polaris Recalls 2023-2024 Sportsman ATVs and Fuel Components Due to Fire Risk

seo.twitterDescription: Polaris recalls 2023-2024 Sportsman ATVs due to fire risks. Check if your vehicle is affected and learn how to get a free repair.

recalls.Description: Peace Industry Group has announced a recall for their "Peace Sports" 125CC Utility and Sport Youth All-Terrain Vehicles (ATVs), specifically models 512 CY125ATV-1 and 518 CY125ATV-6. These vehicles feature the "Peace Sports" branding on the handlebar pad and the sides, with the model number displayed on the VIN plate located on the lower front frame. A notice indicating compliance with the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission's Action Plan is also present on the ATVs.
recalls.Title: Peace Industry Group Issues Recall for Youth ATVs Due to Serious Injury Risks and Safety Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Peace Industry Group at 888-855-6031 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.peacesports.com/ATV-Recall or www.peacesports.com and select "RECALL" at the top of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Peace Sports 512 CY125ATV-1 Utility Youth ATV
Recalled Peace Sports 518 CY125ATV-6 Sport Youth ATV
Recalled Peace Sports Youth ATV VIN plate

Manufacturers.Name: Zhejiang Changying Car Industry Co. LTD, of China

Retailers.Name: AutoWorx Sales LLC, Daryl's Motorcycle Service, Hialeah Scooters, LJS Repairs LLC, M&M Small Engines, Marobo Motors, Motor Werks LLC, One Stop Automotive Repair LLC, Scooters & Small Engine Repairs, Solano Cycles, Unique Motorsports, Wild Hogs Scooters and Motorsports LLC, and other authorized dealerships nationwide from April 2019 through March 2021 for about $1,110.

Importers.Name: Peace Industry Group, of Norcross, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Failure to meet federal ATV safety standards, potential laceration risk from handlebars, inadequate parking brakes leading to collision risk, absence of required safety reflectors, and footguard hazards on model 518 CY125ATV-6.

Remedies.Name: Immediate cessation of use and full refund from Peace Industry Group.

seo.metaDescription: Peace Industry Group recalls youth ATVs due to serious injury risks and safety violations. Contact for refunds and more information.

seo.twitterDescription: Peace Industry Group recalls youth ATVs due to serious injury risks and safety violations. Contact for refunds and more information.

recalls.Description: Textron Specialized Vehicles has announced a recall for Tracker Off Road OX EV vehicles with serial numbers ranging from 8029703 to 8031430. These serial numbers can be found beneath the charger receptacle. The recall is due to a potential fire hazard caused by water infiltration into the lithium-ion battery pack.
recalls.Title: Textron Specialized Vehicles Issues Recall for Tracker Off Road OX EV Light Utility Vehicles Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Textron Specialized Vehicles at 888-438-3946 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email msizemore@textron.com or visit https://www.trackeroffroad.com and navigate to the "Recalls" section or directly access https://www.trackeroffroad.com/recalls.html for further details.

Products.Name: Tracker Off Road OX EV Vehicles

Images.Caption: Recalled Tracker Off Road OX EV

Manufacturers.Name: Textron Specialized Vehicles, of Augusta, Georgia

Retailers.Name: Bass Pro Shops, Cabela's stores, and Tracker Marine and off-road dealerships nationwide from January 2023 through December 2023 for between $12,000 and $13,000.

Hazards.Name: Potential fire hazard due to water exposure in the lithium-ion battery pack.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the Tracker OX EV immediately and contact an authorized Tracker Off Road dealer or service center to arrange for a complimentary battery module inspection and repair. Until this service is completed, do not charge the vehicle and ensure it is parked outdoors, at least 15 feet away from any structures or other vehicles. Textron Specialized Vehicles will reach out to all known purchasers.

seo.metaDescription: Textron recalls Tracker Off Road OX EV vehicles due to fire risk from battery water exposure. Contact them for inspection and repair.
seo.ogTitle: Textron Specialized Vehicles Issues Recall for Tracker Off Road OX EV Light Utility Vehicles Due to Fire Risk
seo.ogDescription: Textron recalls Tracker Off Road OX EV vehicles due to fire risk from battery water exposure. Contact them for inspection and repair.
seo.twitterTitle: Textron Specialized Vehicles Issues Recall for Tracker Off Road OX EV Light Utility Vehicles Due to Fire Risk
seo.twitterDescription: Textron recalls Tracker Off Road OX EV vehicles due to fire risk from battery water exposure. Contact them for inspection and repair.

recalls.Description: BRP is recalling specific models from the 2022 and 2023 Can-Am Commander, Defender, and Maverick Trail series that are equipped with HD7 or 700 engines. These side-by-side vehicles, available in various colors, feature the brand and model name displayed on their sides. The recall is prompted by a maintenance schedule error in the operator's guide, which could lead to clutch failure and potential injury from ejected fragments.
recalls.Title: BRP US Issues Recall for Can-Am Side-By-Side Vehicles Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact BRP at 888-272-9222 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Sunday, or visit https://can-am.brp.com/off-road/us/en/resources/contact-us.html. Additional recall information can be found at https://can-am.brp.com/off-road/us/en/owner-zone/safety-recalls.html or by navigating to the "Safety Recalls" section on any page of https://can-am.brp.com/off-road/us/en/models/side-by-side-vehicles.html.

Products.Name: Can-Am Commander Series (2022-2023)
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 14,555 (In addition, about 2,285 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Commander Series MY2022
Recalled Defender Series MY2022
Recalled Maverick Trail Series MY2022
Recalled Maverick Trail Series MY2023
Recalled Defender Series MY2023
Recalled Maverick Trail Series MY2023

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a clutch malfunction. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: BRP Can-Am authorized dealers nationwide from October 2021 through October 2023 for between $13,000 and $36,000.

Hazards.Name: Incorrect maintenance schedule may lead to clutch failure and ejection of fragments, posing serious injury risks.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled vehicles immediately and review the updated maintenance instructions. BRP will send a revised maintenance booklet to registered owners and will contact known purchasers directly.

seo.metaDescription: BRP recalls 2022-2023 Can-Am side-by-side vehicles due to a risk of injury from clutch failure. Immediate action required.
seo.ogTitle: BRP US Issues Recall for Can-Am Side-By-Side Vehicles Due to Injury Risk
